threats.description: Program does not check whether an account has already been initialized. A second initialize call overwrites existing state, allowing an attacker to reset balances or authority.
threats.remediation: Add an is_initialized boolean guard or use Anchor's #[account(init)] which enforces single initialization.

threats.description: An instruction subtracts lamports from a data account without checking the post-transfer balance against the minimum rent-exempt threshold. Validators may garbage-collect the account.
threats.remediation: After any lamport deduction, verify account.lamports() >= Rent::get()?.minimum_balance(data_len). Use Anchor's rent_exempt = enforce constraint.
